w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Finalized Project Requirements & System Design Document

The objective of this project is to develop a custom email filing application that integrates directly with Microsoft Outlook. The application will allow users to manually file emails and their attachments into designated project folders stored on central mapped network drives.
The system will be used by approximately 50 staff members and must provide an intuitive interface within Outlook for filing, managing, and searching emails stored in shared project folders.
The solution will be implemented as a modern Outlook Web Add-in using web technologies and will integrate with a backend service responsible for performing file system operations on network drives.
The application will be implemented using:
Emails will be stored on central mapped network drives used by the organization for project document storage.
The system must integrate directly within the Outlook interface.
A new toolbar section will be added to Outlook containing the following buttons:
• File Email – opens the email filing interface
• Search – opens the email search interface
• Options – opens the user preferences/settings panel
These buttons must be visible when viewing or reading an email.
When a user right-clicks an email in Outlook (Inbox, Sent Items, or other folders), a new menu item will appear.
The system will display the 10 most recently used filing locations.
A predefined folder used for personal email filing.
Opens the full filing interface where users can browse and select filing locations.
The application will allow users to manually file emails.
Emails are filed only when the user selects the File Email command.
There will be no automatic filing in Phase 1.
Emails must be saved in their original .msg format.
To avoid duplicate names or invalid characters, the system will use a standardized naming format:
Invalid characters in email subjects must be sanitized before saving.
Attachments will be stored alongside the email file.
Two storage options may be supported:
Attachments extracted to a structured folder:
The default behaviour will be configurable in user preferences.
Users must be able to select multiple locations and file a single email to multiple folders simultaneously.
If the same email is filed to a location where it already exists, the system must prompt the user with options:
After filing an email successfully, the user must receive visual confirmation indicating that the filing operation completed successfully.
Users must be able to manage filing locations through a dedicated interface.
• Assign location to a collection (e.g., Personal, Projects)
When users click Add Location, a form must appear with the following fields:
Users must also be able to browse for folder locations using a folder selection dialog.
The application must maintain a list of the 10 most recently used filing locations.
These locations will appear automatically in the Suggested Locations menu when users right-click an email.
The system will provide a dedicated interface allowing users to search filed emails stored across network locations.
• Open the stored email directly in Outlook
• View the folder path where the email is stored
• Sort results by date, sender, or subject
The application must provide a settings panel allowing users to configure filing behaviour.
• Move email to Deleted Items after filing
• Prompt user to file Sent Messages
• Filing an email should complete within 2–3 seconds.
• Search results should return within 1–2 seconds when indexed.
The system must ensure secure handling of email data.
• Only authorized users may access designated project folders
• Communication between the Outlook Add-in and backend service must use secure
• Email data must not be stored outside approved network drives.
The system must gracefully handle errors including:
• Communication errors with the local service.
The system must maintain logs for troubleshooting.
The following features will not be included in the first phase:
• Deep content search within attachment files (PDF, Word, etc.)
These features may be considered in future project phases.
To support both Classic Outlook and New Outlook, the system will be developed as a modern Outlook Web Add-in using web technologies.
Because Outlook Web Add-ins run in a sandboxed environment and cannot directly access local or network file systems, an additional service is required to perform file operations.
The Outlook Add-in provides the user interface and integrates directly into Outlook.
A lightweight local service will run on the user’s machine.
• Receive commands from the Outlook Add-in
• Save emails and attachments to network drives
This service acts as a bridge between the Add-in and the network file system.
The system will use Microsoft Graph to retrieve the full MIME content of emails and their attachments.
The Local Agent will process this data and store it in .msg format on the network drive.
Emails will be stored directly on the organization's mapped network drives.
User configuration and system metadata will be stored in:
Searching large network folders can be slow. Therefore, a lightweight indexing system will be used.
The Local Agent will maintain an index containing:
The search interface will query this index to provide fast results without scanning all folders.
The application will include the following main screens:
The system will support approximately 50 concurrent users, all accessing shared project folders on network drives.
The application must remain responsive and stable under this load.

This document defines the finalized functional and system requirements for Milestone 1 of the Outlook Email Filing Application. These requirements establish the scope, architecture, and operational behaviour of the system to ensure reliable email filing, efficient search functionality, and seamless integration with Outlook.
Future milestones will focus on implementation
